1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おたいあかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. 1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

1140005012125 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市小田井町15番地6', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小田井縣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655259}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Odai Agata Shrine |画像 = [[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

[[File:007-440x326.jpg|270px|Haiden and honden]]
Haiden and honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Odai-cho 15-6 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} year 11, {{ill|lt=March 14|Q2404|WD=Q2404|1=March 14}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyooka Yanagi Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} on August 1 and 2 each year |札所等 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Odai
Agata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Odai1.jpg|thumb|Torii of Odai Agata Shrine]] '''Odai Agata Shrine''' (おだいあがたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine
(Haraedo four-pillar deities) File:004-440x329.jpg|Yanagi-no-miya
(five sons and three daughters deities) File:006-220x164.jpg|Inari Shrine
(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal. No parking lot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === Sources / references === Hashimoto Tashichi (ed.), ''Toyooka Guide'', Tajima Shimbunsha, Taisho 1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/913755 (accessed 2025-06-17) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === * [https://www.odai-agata-shrine.org/ Odai Agata Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322003.html Odai Agata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Odai Agata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

A subordinate shrine of Odai Agata Shrine, "Yanaginomiya Shrine," is associated with the "Toyooka Yanagi Festival," held every year on August 1 and 2. It is the largest festival in the central urban area of Toyooka. It is regarded as one of the five shrines of Tajima. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifuden'' records three seats: upper seat Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto, middle seat Amateru-kuni-teruhiko Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, lower seat Watatsumi deity. === History === In ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Kojiki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District traditions) and ''Kokushi Monjo / Tajima Jinja Keifu Denki'' (Vol. 4, Kinosaki District shrine genealogy), the following tradition appears: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} went west into Tajima and stayed at Suganomiya. He descended to Toyooka plain and opened sacred rice fields. He also ordered Tarui Ame-Mononobe-no-Mikoto to dig a sacred spring at Kinosaki and irrigate the fields, producing auspicious rice. Therefore the land was called Toyooka-hara, and the sacred well was called Oda-i (sacred paddy well), later becoming Odai; this is why the district was called Odai Agata. In the 3rd year, autumn, month 8 of Emperor Jinmu, Kunitsukuri Onamuchi-no-Mikoto was enshrined on a hill at Kinosaki. In month 9 of the same year, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto was also enshrined on the same hill, and Homae-itsukinushi-no-Mikoto served before the food offerings. In regency year 3 of Empress Jingu, for merit in the Korean campaign, shrine buildings were constructed, offerings dedicated, and rites performed. The Agata governor of Kinosaki, Takemimono-no-Mikoto, served as envoy of offerings and enshrined the Great Watatsumi deities (Sokotsu, Nakatsu, and Uwatsu) in the lower seat. In Hakuhō 3 of Emperor Tenmu (summer, month 6), when a great drought struck the realm, rain was prayed for at Odai Agata Shrine, ritual implements were placed in the shrine storehouse, and for the first time the Hokotate rite and Mitoage rite were performed; later these came to be called "Hokotate Shinji" and "Kawachi Shinji." Also, the sacred sake place was ritually established, sake was brewed, food deities were worshipped, food offerings were prepared and presented. The Sakatare deity ({{ill|lt=Sakatare Shrine|Q11644306|WD=Q11644306|1=Sakatare Shrine}}) and Miketsu deity in Minie village (present Mie) are identified with these. The Kinosaki district governor Mononobe Karakuni no Muraji Kugui presided over this. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730|1=842}}), October: official shrine * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=July 27|Q2719|WD=Q2719|1=July 27}}: Senior Sixth Rank * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|WD=Q25061|1=868}}), {{ill|lt=December 27|Q2800|WD=Q2800|1=December 27}}: Junior Fifth Rank Upper * {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239|1=905}}): listed as shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Genko|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}}), summer: Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|WD=Q6688|1=1575}}), October: burned in the Battle of Noda * Tensho era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}): Hideyoshi camped at the shrine and confiscated shrine lands * {{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} era ({{ill|lt=1684|Q7654|WD=Q7654|1=1684}}-{{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668|1=1687}}): shrine buildings revived * {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}-{{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006|1=1740}}): reconstruction * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), October: became prefectural shrine * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}): moved to current site due to Maruyama River flood-control works === Subsidiary shrines === File:003-440x325.jpg|Kawashimo Shrine

(Toyotoka-hime-no-Mikoto) === Festivals === * "Toyooka Yanagi Festival" === Site information === ; Access ''On foot'' * From {{ill|lt=Toyooka Station (Hyogo)|Q871612|WD=Q871612|lt=Toyooka Station|1=Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)}} (JR West San'in Main Line), 1,400 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} east-northeast * From {{ill|lt=Kounotori-no-sato Station|Q7676863|WD=Q7676863|1=Kōnotori-no-sato Station}} (Kyoto Tango Railway Miyatoyo Line), 1,500 meters west-southwest ''By car'' * Head toward Toyooka and go north along National Route 312 by the Maruyama River; at the north edge of the urban area, below the west side of the road at the Horikawa-bashi signal.
